attacking_style: Long-range breakaway specialist, willing to go solo from 160km out. Targets early breakaway placement in hilly/undulating stages rather than attacking on major climbs. Works breakaway-to-selection tactics rather than pure climbing duels.
team_tactics: Team supports long breakaway attempts even when mathematically futile for sponsor exposure. Used as high-value domestique on final climbs for team leaders when not given breakaway freedom.
terrain_preference: Hilly stage finales and undulating Monument-style parcours (6th Il Lombardia). Survives selection races better than pure mountain stages. Diesel engine suits long efforts over explosive climbing.
vulnerability: Gets distanced on pure climbing efforts (dropped then regrouped at Paris-Nice). Not a finisher from reduced groups - relies on breakaway survival rather than final kick or summit finish punch.
key_patterns: Breakaway podiums in World Tour: 3rd Dauphiné, 6th Lombardia from early moves (2 confirmations)
Exceptional endurance ceiling: 160km solo, paced Quintana group on GT climb (2 confirmations)
Team exploits his diesel engine for exposure plays, supporting futile-but-visible long efforts
Better Monument/hilly results than pure mountain stages; selection racing suits him more
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 observation shows regression to chase group survival vs 2019-2020 breakaway success; needs more recent data
